meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'can't finish projects' cycle by building passive income AI apps. A student-focused guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship fast.
content.answer_block: The secret to finishing projects isn't discipline; it's momentum. By building micro-SaaS apps focused on passive income, you align your code with tangible results. Use Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting and Antigravity to scaffold production-ready architecture, turning incomplete drafts into finished, revenue-generating assets that validate your skills.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: a graveyard of GitHub repositories, half-finished READMEs, and the crushing weight of 'feature creep.' You start with passion, but the complexity of production-ready code eventually stalls your momentum. You aren't failing; you're just building without a feedback loop that rewards your effort.

Passive income is the antidote. When your code generates value, the motivation to finish shifts from 'completing a chore' to 'maintaining an asset.' We’ll use modern AI tooling to bypass the boilerplate and focus on the architecture that actually ships. It’s time to stop collecting unfinished ideas and start building a portfolio that pays.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Problem
steps.description: Identify a small, repeatable task that people pay to solve. Don't build a platform; build a utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this niche: [Insert Niche]. Generate a 3-feature MVP spec that solves a specific pain point and can be monetized via Stripe API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md outlining the MVP features and the database schema for a user-payment flow.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular structure so you don't get lost in your own files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 project with Tailwind CSS, Supabase authentication, and a Stripe webhook handler. Ensure the file structure is optimized for scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project directory and install necessary dependencies based on the Antigravity scaffold.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Focus only on the primary function. If it doesn't earn money or solve the core problem, cut it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core logic for the [Feature Name]. Keep the code functional, modular, and include error handling for edge cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/app/api/process/route.ts --prompt 'Implement the core logic we just generated and ensure it follows clean code principles.'

steps.title: Integrate Payments
steps.description: Connect your app to Stripe to turn your code into a potential revenue stream.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe checkout session handler. Include logic to update the Supabase user table upon successful payment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the Stripe webhook handler and test the payment flow with a mock event.'

steps.title: Final Polish & Deployment
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel and set up observability to monitor your app's performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment checklist for a Vercel production build, including environment variable management and security headers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review the codebase for security vulnerabilities and prepare the project for production deployment.'

pro_tips: Always build the payment flow first; it forces you to define what the user is actually paying for.
Limit your MVP to 48 hours of total development time to prevent the 'perfection trap'.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests as you go; it prevents technical debt from accumulating.
If you find yourself stuck on a UI component for more than an hour, swap it for a pre-built component library like Shadcn/UI.
Document your 'wins' as you build—these snippets become the case studies that land you high-paying freelance work.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, set up a simple landing page with a waitlist or payment link. Share it with 10 people in your target niche to gather feedback, then iterate based on real usage data.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint. Now you need the environment to grow your skills and accountability to ensure you ship. Don't let another project sit idle in your drafts folder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
